title: Test Forms and Tables across Failure States
summary: Build and verify form table testing in release-readiness evidence matrix through prediction, inspection, explanation, cumulative code, and changed-case transfer.
objectives: Explain form table testing from purpose, behavior, and evidence.
Apply form table testing without discarding earlier valid work.
Diagnose the named misconception in changed content, input, and failure states.

steps.title: 01 · Predict the release-readiness evidence matrix case · theory
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 1: Which claim preserves form table testing when data, input, and layout change? Scenario: release-readiness evidence matrix.
steps.why: Test Forms and Tables across Failure States makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 1 in release-readiness evidence matrix.

content.text: For Test Forms and Tables across Failure States, choose the form table testing claim that preserves the release-readiness evidence matrix purpose, native behavior, and understandable relationships when presentation changes.

hints: Test Forms and Tables across Failure States checkpoint 1: identify the user task and data relationship first.

Test Forms and Tables across Failure States checkpoint 1: make the smallest repair that preserves earlier passing work.

options.text: Release-readiness evidence matrix case 1: successful mouse submission proves the form is accessible and robust.
Release-readiness evidence matrix case 1: run a written matrix across input methods, viewport changes, validation failures, and announced relationships, then keep the evidence.
Release-readiness evidence matrix case 1: inspect only the default desktop state because responsive CSS does not affect semantics.

options.text: release-readiness evidence matrix phase 4.1: define success and failure cases for controls and representative data cells
release-readiness evidence matrix phase 4.2: run keyboard, zoom, narrow-width, validation, and semantic checks
release-readiness evidence matrix phase 4.3: record source, behavior, announcement, and recovery evidence
release-readiness evidence matrix phase 4.4: repair the earliest cause and rerun the complete affected matrix

options.text: Release-readiness evidence matrix case 5: keep the release-readiness evidence matrix defect because the happy path worked once.

Release-readiness evidence matrix case 5: a plausible success state is incomplete evidence. Run a written matrix across input methods, viewport changes, validation failures, and announced relationships, then keep the evidence.
Release-readiness evidence matrix case 5: replace the complete artifact before isolating the failed relationship.

steps.title: 06 · Read the release-readiness evidence matrix case · theory
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 6: Which claim preserves form table testing when data, input, and layout change? Scenario: release-readiness evidence matrix.
steps.why: Test Forms and Tables across Failure States makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 6 in release-readiness evidence matrix.

content.text: One successful mouse submission proves little. Robust forms and tables need keyboard-only completion, visible focus, zoom and reflow, narrow-width inspection, valid and invalid data, error recovery, source and accessibility-tree checks, and representative screen-reader semantics. Record evidence and retest after each repair. Apply it to release-readiness evidence matrix.

hints: Test Forms and Tables across Failure States checkpoint 6: identify the user task and data relationship first.
Test Forms and Tables across Failure States checkpoint 6: compare source, native behavior, and announced meaning.
Test Forms and Tables across Failure States checkpoint 6: make the smallest repair that preserves earlier passing work.

options.text: Release-readiness evidence matrix case 6: successful mouse submission proves the form is accessible and robust.
Release-readiness evidence matrix case 6: run a written matrix across input methods, viewport changes, validation failures, and announced relationships, then keep the evidence.
Release-readiness evidence matrix case 6: inspect only the default desktop state because responsive CSS does not affect semantics.

steps.title: 07 · Defend the release-readiness evidence matrix decision · theory

steps.why: Test Forms and Tables across Failure States makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 7 in release-readiness evidence matrix.

content.title: release-readiness evidence matrix case note
content.text: Defend form table testing in release-readiness evidence matrix: connect this user task to source evidence, native behavior, failure behavior, and the final retest.
